proof: function[T0](x0: T0 -> Bool, x1: T0) { x1 ∈ Set.new[T0](x0) = x0(x1) }[Real](is_limit_point_of_set(s), x)
function(x0: Set[Real]) { Set.new[Real](derived_set_contains(x0)) = derived_set(x0) }(s)
function(x0: Set[Real], x1: Real) { is_adherent_point_of_set(x0 ∖ Set.singleton(x1), x1) = is_limit_point_of_set(x0, x1) }(s, x)
function[T0](x0: Set[T0], x1: Set[T0]) { forall(x2: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or x2 ∈ x1 } = x0 ⊂ x1 }[Real](derived_set(s), closure(s))
let w0: Real satisfy { w0 ∈ derived_set(s) and not w0 ∈ closure(s) }
function(x0: Set[Real], x1: Real) { not x1 ∈ derived_set(x0) or x1 ∈ closure(x0) }(s, w0)

goal: is_closed_set(s) = forall(x0: Real) { is_adherent_point_of_set(s, x0) implies s.contains(x0) }
proof: function(x0: Set[Real]) { forall(x1: Real) { not is_adherent_point_of_set(x0, x1) or x1 ∈ x0 } = is_closed_set(x0) }(s)
function(x0: Set[Real], x1: Real) { not is_closed_set(x0) or not is_adherent_point_of_set(x0, x1) or x1 ∈ x0 }(s, x)
function[T0](x0: Set[T0], x1: Set[T0]) { forall(x2: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or x2 ∈ x1 } = x0 ⊂ x1 }[Real](derived_set(s), s)
function[T0](x0: Set[T0], x1: Set[T0]) { forall(x2: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or x2 ∈ x1 } = x0 ⊂ x1 }[Real](s ∖ Set.singleton(x), t ∖ Set.singleton(x))
function(x0: Set[Real], x1: Real) { is_adherent_point_of_set(x0 ∖ Set.singleton(x1), x1) = is_limit_point_of_set(x0, x1) }(s, x)
let w0: Real satisfy { w0.is_positive and not is_eps_adherent_to_set(t ∖ Set.singleton(x), x, w0) }
function(x0: Real) { not x0.is_positive or is_eps_adherent_to_set(t ∖ Set.singleton(x), x, x0) }(w0)
function(x0: Set[Real], x1: Real) { is_adherent_point_of_set(x0 ∖ Set.singleton(x1), x1) = is_limit_point_of_set(x0, x1) }(t, x)
function[T0](x0: Set[T0], x1: Set[T0]) { forall(x2: T0) { not x2 ∈ x0 or x2 ∈ x1 } = x0 ⊂ x1 }[Real](derived_set(s), derived_set(t))

goal: is_isolated_point_of_set(s, x) = s.contains(x) and exists(k0: Real) { k0.is_positive and forall(x0: Real) { s.contains(x0) and x0 != x implies not x0.is_close(x, k0) } }
proof: function(x0: Set[Real], x1: Real) { (x1 ∈ x0 and exists(k0: Real) { k0.is_positive and forall(x2: Real) { not x2 ∈ x0 or x1 = x2 or not x2.is_close(x1, k0) } }) = is_isolated_point_of_set(x0, x1) }(s, x)

goal: exists(k0: Real) { k0.is_positive and forall(x0: Real) { s.contains(x0) and x0 != x implies not x0.is_close(x, k0) } }

proof: function(x0: Set[Real], x1: Real) { is_adherent_point_of_set(x0 ∖ Set.singleton(x1), x1) = is_limit_point_of_set(x0, x1) }(s, x)
function(x0: Set[Real], x1: Real, x2: Real) { not is_eps_adherent_to_set(x0, x1, x2) or exists(k0: Real) { k0 ∈ x0 and k0.is_close(x1, x2) } }(s ∖ Set.singleton(x), x, eps)
y ∈ s ∖ Set.singleton(x)
(forall(x0: Real) { not x0 ∈ s or x0 = x or not x0.is_close(x, eps) } = true)
function[T0](x0: T0) { Set.singleton[T0](x0).contains = function(x1: T0) { x0 = x1 } }[Real](x)
y ∈ s and not y ∈ Set.singleton(x)
function(x0: Real) { not (x0 ∈ s and x0 != x and x0.is_close(x, eps)) }(y)
